statement.markdown: Given a binary bitmask of length `n`, recover the lost bits by shifting the 1-bits to the right and left to fill all gaps between 1-bits. You should not shift any 0-bits in the process, and no two 1-bits should be adjacent after shifting. The result should be the bitmask with all lost bits recovered.
statement.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer `n`, the length of the binary bitmask. The second line contains a string of `n` binary digits representing the bitmask.
statement.outputFormat: The recovered bitmask as a string of `n` binary digits.
statement.notes: The binary bitmask is 0-indexed, so the first bit is at index 0. The input bitmask might contain multiple consecutive 0-bits between two 1-bits.

examples.explanation: The original bitmask has 3 lost bits: 1-0-1. After shifting 1-bits to the right and left, the recovered bitmask is 111111.
The original bitmask has no lost bits.

hints.text: Use a greedy approach to find the next 1-bit to shift and fill the gaps.

editorial.title: Bitmask Recovery
editorial.intuition: To recover lost bits, we need to fill the gaps between 1-bits without shifting any 0-bits. We can achieve this by maintaining a list of the indices of 1-bits in the bitmask.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach involves checking all possible combinations of shifting 1-bits to fill the gaps, which is inefficient.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use a greedy approach to keep track of the 1-bits and shift them to fill the gaps efficiently.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves iterating through the bitmask, maintaining a list of 1-bit indices, and shifting the 1-bits to fill the gaps while avoiding adjacent 1-bits.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proved by showing that it correctly recovers lost bits without shifting any 0-bits.
editorial.dryRun: Perform a dry run on the original bitmask to understand the greedy approach.
commonMistakes: Not maintaining a list of 1-bit indices

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: string recoverBits(string& bitmask, int n); };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public static String recoverBits(String bitmask, int n) { } }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def recoverBits(self, bitmask: str, n: int) -> str: pass

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: string recoverBits(string& bitmask, int n) { string result = bitmask; for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) { if (bitmask[i] == '1') { result += bitmask[i]; } } return result; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public static String recoverBits(String bitmask, int n) { StringBuilder result = new StringBuilder(bitmask); for (int i = 1; i < n; i++) { if (bitmask.charAt(i) == '1') { result.append(bitmask.charAt(i)); } } return result.toString(); } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def recoverBits(self, bitmask: str, n: int) -> str: result = bitmask; for i in range(1, n): if bitmask[i] == '1': result += bitmask[i]; return result;
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function recoverBits(bitmask, n) { let result = bitmask; for (let i = 1; i < n; i++) { if (bitmask[i] == '1') { result += bitmask[i]; } } return result; }

functionSignature.python: def recoverBits(self, bitmask: str, n: int) -> str:

solutions.cpp: class Solution { public: string recoverBits(string& bitmask, int n) { string result = ''; for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { if (bitmask[i] == '1') { result += '1'; } else if (!result.empty() && result.back() == '1') { result += '1'; } } return result; } };
